title: Asola Bhatti Wildlife Sanctuary
tagline: Asola Bhatti Wildlife Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Delhi

overview.short: Asola-Bhati Wildlife Sanctuary covering 32.71 km2 area on the Southern Delhi Ridge of Aravalli hill range on Delhi-Haryana border lies in Southern Delhi as well as northern parts o
overview.description: Asola-Bhati Wildlife Sanctuary covering 32.71 km2 area on the Southern Delhi Ridge of Aravalli hill range on Delhi-Haryana border lies in Southern Delhi as well as northern parts of Faridabad and Gurugram districts of Haryana state. Biodiversity significance of Ridge lies in its merger with the Indo-Gangetic Plain, as it is the part of the Northern Aravalli leopard wildlife corridor, an important wildlife corridor which starts from the Sariska National Park in Rajasthan, passes through Nuh, Faridabad and Gurugram districts of Haryana and ends at Delhi Ridge. This protected area contains one of

topPlaces.name: Tilpath Valley Biodiversity Park
topPlaces.description: Tilpath Valley Biodiversity Park, is a 172 acre biodiversity area in the South Delhi Ridge within the Northern Aravalli Leopard Wildlife Corridor, located northwest of the Asola Bhatti Wildlife Sanctuary.

topPlaces.name: Tigri, Delhi
topPlaces.description: Tigri is a census town, near Khanpur in South district in the National Capital Territory of Delhi, India. In 2012 Arwind Kejriwal Launched a 'Bijli-Paani Satyagraha' (protest against the tariff hike of electricity and water), Kejriwal-led India Against Corruption (IAC) 'restored' power supply to a laborer's house in south Delhi's Tigri.

topPlaces.name: Deoli, Delhi
topPlaces.description: Deoli also, (Devli) is a census town in the South district of the state of Delhi, India.

topPlaces.name: Satbari
topPlaces.description: Satbari (also spelled as Satbadi) is a village and census town located in the Chhatarpur area of South Delhi district in Delhi, India. Its postal code is 110074.

topPlaces.name: Khanpur, Delhi
topPlaces.description: Khanpur is a neighborhood in Delhi, India, situated in the South Delhi district, on the Mehrauli- Badarpur road. Its surrounding localities are Saket, Madangir, Sainik farms, Ambedkar Nagar, plus Indo-Tibetan Border Police's (ITBP) National Centre for UNCIVPOL training is also situated at Tigri in Khanpur.

topPlaces.name: Sainik Farm
topPlaces.description: Sainik Farm is an affluent settlement in New Delhi where prominent people reside. The area has been embroiled in legal trouble over its legitimacy.

topPlaces.name: Dr. Karni Singh Shooting Range
topPlaces.description: Dr. Karni Singh Shooting Range is a shooting range in New Delhi, India.

topPlaces.name: Anangpur Dam
topPlaces.description: The Anangpur Dam is an Indian hydraulic engineering structure said to be built during the reign of King Anangpal I of the Tomara dynasty in the 8th century. It is located near the Anangpur village in Faridabad district, Haryana, India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Asola Bhatti Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Asola Bhatti Wildlife Sanctuary is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Asola Bhatti Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Indira Gandhi International Airport, Delhi (16 km) and the nearest railway station is New Delhi Railway Station (19 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Asola Bhatti Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Asola Bhatti Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Asola Bhatti Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Asola Bhatti Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Asola Bhatti Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Tilpath Valley Biodiversity Park, Tigri, Delhi, Deoli, Delhi, Satbari, Khanpur, Delhi.
